title: The Binomial Distribution
description: The binomial distribution X ~ B(n, p) as a model for counting successes in a fixed number of independent trials, including the probability formula, cumulative probabilities, and the mean and variance.
objectives: Recognise when a binomial model X ~ B(n, p) is appropriate, and state its conditions
Use the formula P(X = r) = C(n, r) p^r (1-p)^(n-r) to calculate individual binomial probabilities by hand
Calculate cumulative probabilities P(X ≤ r) and P(X ≥ r) for small n, using the complement rule where helpful
State and apply the mean np and variance np(1-p) of a binomial distribution

lessons.title: Mean and variance of a binomial distribution
